perturbation.original_text: The Reseller shall comply at all times with all applicable laws, rules, regulations, and industry standards relating to the storage, packaging, marketing, distribution, laboratory work, and sale of the Products in the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: The Reseller shall endeavor to comply with all applicable laws, rules, regulations, and industry standards relating to the storage, packaging, marketing, distribution, laboratory work, and sale of the Products in the Territory.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'shall comply' with 'shall endeavor to comply' introduces ambiguity. 'Endeavor' implies an attempt, not a guarantee, potentially allowing the Reseller to fall short of full legal compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Israel Ministry of Health Regulations regarding Medical Device Storage and Handling
perturbation.law_explanation: Israeli Medical Devices Law, section 21, mandates strict adherence to storage and handling regulations for medical devices to ensure their safety and efficacy. The modified text, using 'endeavor to comply,' weakens this mandatory obligation, potentially leading to non-compliance with the detailed requirements specified by the Ministry of Health regarding temperature control, labeling, and record-keeping. The first URL is to the english landing page of the law, the second URL leads to the full translation of the law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act) | FDA Skip to main content Skip to FDA Search Skip to in this section menu Skip to footer links In this section : Laws Enforced by FDA Home Regulatory Information Laws Enforced by FDA Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act) Laws Enforced by FDA FD&C Act Reference Information United States Code, Title 21 The laws of the United States are organized by subject into the United States Code. The United States Code contains only the currently enacted statutory language. The official United States Code is maintained by the Office of the Law Revision Counsel in the United States House of Representatives. The Office of the Law Revision Counsel reviews enacted laws and determines where the statutory language should be codified related to its topic. The Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act and subsequent amending statutes are codified into Title 21 Chapter 9 of the United States Code. The listing of FD&C Act sections presented here identifies both the FD&C Act and U.S. Code section numbers, which can be used to narrow your search on the Law Revision Counsel website. To search the FD&C Act on the Law Revision Counsel website, use the Advanced Search to limit the your search to Title 21. To search the FD&C Act on the Law Revision Counsel website, you may either search by U.S. code section number or browse the Title 21 section listing . Content current as of: 03/29/2018 Back to Top
perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates strict compliance with all applicable laws and regulations. The modified text weakens this obligation by changing 'shall comply' to 'shall endeavor to comply.' This introduces ambiguity regarding the reseller's responsibility, as 'endeavor' suggests an attempt rather than a guarantee of full compliance. The Law Explanation highlights that specific laws, such as Israeli Medical Devices Law section 21, require strict adherence to storage and handling regulations. The modification could lead to non-compliance with these detailed requirements. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 speaks generally about the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act), the core issue is not the specific law but the principle that legal compliance should be mandatory, not discretionary. By softening the compliance requirement, the modified text creates a legal contradiction by allowing for potential non-compliance.

perturbation.original_text: Todos is responsible for obtaining AMAR approval from the Israeli Ministry of Health.
perturbation.changed_text: Todos will attempt to obtain AMAR approval from the Israeli Ministry of Health.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'is responsible' to 'will attempt' introduces uncertainty regarding whether AMAR approval will be secured. This shifts a definite obligation to a weaker commitment.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Israel Ministry of Health regulations regarding medical device marketing
perturbation.law_explanation: The Public Health Ordinance [New Version], 1940, Section 56 requires medical devices to be registered and approved before marketing in Israel. 'Attempting' to get AMAR approval does not guarantee that Todos will actually obtain it. The new wording contradicts the requirement for pre-market approval, potentially leading to the illegal marketing of unapproved medical devices. The first URL points to the Ministry of Health website about Medical Devices and the second URL contains the text of the Public Health Ordinance.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: NATLEX - - Skip to Main Content Home Breadcrumb Title_of_text Main Region First Region Country(ies) Adopted on (Date of text)
perturbation.justification: The change from 'is responsible' to 'will attempt' weakens the obligation to obtain AMAR approval. The Law Explanation highlights that Section 56 of the Public Health Ordinance requires medical devices to be registered and approved before marketing in Israel. 'Attempting' does not guarantee compliance with this requirement. Although Scraped Law Snippet 1 is missing and Scraped Law Snippet 2 is incomplete, the Law Explanation, Perturbation Explanation, and comparison of the original and changed texts support the conclusion that a contradiction exists. The change introduces ambiguity, making it difficult to enforce the requirement for pre-market approval and potentially leading to the illegal marketing of unapproved devices. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities Legal Contradiction' definition, because it makes the obligation ambiguous, and could result in non-compliance.

perturbation.original_text: Todos shall fulfill with reasonable dispatch all orders received from the Reseller and accepted by Todos.
perturbation.changed_text: Todos shall aim to fulfill orders received from the Reseller and accepted by Todos.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'fulfill with reasonable dispatch' with 'aim to fulfill' significantly weakens the obligation. 'Aim' suggests a goal, not a commitment to timely fulfillment, potentially leading to delays that harm the Reseller's ability to meet market demand.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 41 of the Contract Law (General Part) requires contracts to be performed in good faith and in a customary manner. The modification, changing 'fulfill with reasonable dispatch' to 'aim to fulfill', provides an escape clause that may conflict with the concept of good faith and timely contractual performance as understood under Israeli law. The first URL is a Wikipedia page explaining the basics of Israeli contract law while the second is a Globes.co.il page about a similar case about good faith in contracts.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-207. Additional Terms in Acceptance or Confirmation. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-207. Additional Terms in Acceptance or Confirmation. (1) A definite and seasonable expression of acceptance or a written confirmation which is sent within a reasonable time operates as an acceptance even though it states terms additional to or different from those offered or agreed upon, unless acceptance is expressly made conditional on assent to the additional or different terms. (2) The additional terms are to be construed as proposals for addition to the contract . Between merchants such terms become part of the contract unless: (a) the offer expressly limits acceptance to the terms of the offer; (b) they materially alter it; or (c) notification of objection to them has already been given or is given within a reasonable time after notice of them is received. (3) Conduct by both parties which recognizes the existence of a contract is sufficient to establish a contract for sale although the writings of the parties do not otherwise establish a contract. In such case the terms of the particular contract consist of those terms on which the writings of the parties agree, together with any supplementary terms incorporated under any other provisions of this Act. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modification from 'fulfill with reasonable dispatch' to 'aim to fulfill' introduces ambiguity regarding the obligation to fulfill orders promptly. This ambiguity creates a legal contradiction under the definition provided, where ambiguities in legal obligations can lead to difficulties in enforcement. The first scraped law snippet, while pertaining to the UCC, highlights the importance of definite terms in contracts. While the specific law cited by the LLM isn't directly provided, the change weakens the original commitment, potentially leading to disputes and making the obligation difficult to enforce, fulfilling the criteria for a contradiction.
